demographics.living_situation: shares a home with a roommate

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I felt completely fine, then the room just went grey, I woke up on the kitchen floor, and the back of my head is throbbing.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I passed out and hit my head and my roommate is really worried — I want to know if I need to get it checked tonight.

symptom_narrative.onset: It happened suddenly about half an hour ago while I was standing at the bench making dinner.
symptom_narrative.duration: I was only out for maybe 30 seconds and I felt back to normal really quickly.
symptom_narrative.character: Just before it happened everything went grey and I felt lightheaded, then the next thing I knew I was on the floor. My roommate said I went really pale and just slid down.
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: The fainting itself is over, but my head is throbbing where I hit it and I feel a bit shaken and anxious.
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: The sore spot is the back of my head. There's a small tender lump there.
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: It was a one-off. I've never fainted like this before.
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: Lying down and resting on the sofa has helped and I feel clear-headed now.
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: Nothing that I can tell — it just came on out of nowhere.
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I feel a bit thirsty and my mouth is dry. I don't have any chest pain and my heart doesn't feel like it's racing.
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: No, this is the first time anything like this has happened.
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm up and talking normally now, just resting because my roommate wants to keep an eye on me.

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I'd been standing in one spot in a warm kitchen for a good 45 minutes cooking. I don't think I drank much water today.

objective_data_offered.value: Roommate says: 'She went really pale, her legs just gave way and she slid down to the floor. She was out cold for maybe half a minute and then came round and knew where she was straight away.'
objective_data_offered.timing: witnessed at the time of the collapse ~30 minutes ago

objective_data_offered.value: On video the patient is alert, sitting up and talking in full sentences, normal skin colour now, no facial droop or slurred speech.
There is a small tender lump on the back of my head where I hit the floor; the skin isn't cut and it isn't bleeding.
I didn't bite my tongue, I didn't wet myself, and I wasn't confused or drowsy afterwards.
Roommate reports the kitchen was warm and the patient had been standing at the bench cooking for about 45 minutes.
